title: OOM of clickhouse-server  on `Aggregation::mergeBlocks`
description:
### Company or project name
_No response_
### Describe what's wrong
OOMKiller kills Clickhouse server, due to huge RAM consumption.
### Does it reproduce on the most recent release?
Yes
### How to reproduce
```
CREATE TABLE test (ts Date, a String, b String, c String, d String) ENGINE= MergeTree() ORDER BY a;
INSERT INTO test SELECT today() - rand32()%100, toString(rand32()%100), toString(rand32()%100), toString(rand32()%100), toString(rand32()%100) FROM numbers(10000000000)
SELECT
ifNull(fun_res, 0),
count(*)
FROM
(
SELECT
a,
windowFunnel(86400000, 'strict_increase', 'strict_once')((b = '10') OR (c = '10'), (b = '22') OR (c = '33'), (b = '62') OR (c = '55'), (b = '6') OR (c = '91'), (b = '9') OR (c = '91'), (b = '99') OR (c = '99'), (b = '74') OR (c = '74'), (b = '55') OR (c = '55'), (b = '13') OR (c = '13'), (b = '69') OR (c = '69')) AS fun_res
FROM test
GROUP BY a
)
GROUP BY fun_res
SETTINGS log_queries = 1, max_memory_usage = 28991029248
Query id: 9b5c5f0f-0302-46b0-8b41-7b7e4d22649f
Error on processing query: Code: 32. DB::Exception: Attempt to read after eof: while receiving packet from localhost:9001, ::1, local address: [::1]:37012. (ATTEMPT_TO_READ_AFTER_EOF) (version 25.8.11.66 (official build))
Connecting to localhost:9001 as user _admin.
Code: 210. DB::NetException: Connection reset by peer, while reading from socket (peer: [::1]:9001, local: [::1]:35742): (localhost:9001, ::1, local address: [::1]:35742). (NETWORK_ERROR)
```
Note: it is 30GB of RAM on my machine, maybe for repo more should be inserted.
### Expected behavior
_No response_
### Error message and/or stacktrace
_No response_
### Additional context
Actually right before OOM, memory usage of the query is much more than `max_memory_usage`:
```
is_initial_query:      1
user:                  _admin
query_id:              8c277828-7411-475d-bf79-f109bc79fee2
address:               ::1
port:                  40418
initial_user:          _admin
initial_query_id:      8c277828-7411-475d-bf79-f109bc79fee2

interface:             1
os_user:               mburdukov

bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_1 (ts DATE, a INT, b FLOAT, c VARCHAR(255), d TIMESTAMP) ENGINE= MergeTree() ORDER BY a;

INSERT INTO test_1 SELECT CURRENT_DATE - MOD(RAND(), 100), MOD(RAND(), 100), CASE WHEN MOD(RAND(), 2) = 0 THEN CAST(MOD(RAND(), 100) AS CHAR) ELSE NULL END, NOW() FROM dual CONNECT BY LEVEL <= 100000;
CREATE TABLE test_2 (id BIGINT, value VARCHAR(255), created_at TIMESTAMP DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP) ENGINE= MergeTree() ORDER BY id;
INSERT INTO test_2 SELECT LEVEL, CAST(MOD(RAND(), 100) AS CHAR) FROM dual CONNECT BY LEVEL <= 1000000;
SELECT a, COUNT(*) FROM (SELECT a, CASE WHEN b > 50 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS fun_res FROM test_1 GROUP BY a) GROUP BY fun_res;
key_bug_features: High RAM consumption during Aggregation::mergeBlocks operation
